title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While hiding in a closet, Bondell observes a secret meeting of individuals wearing hourglass masks. The participants include a bearded Russian named Mosgolovsky, who acts as the host; an American with an Irish lilt identified as "four o'clock"; an elegant, tall man identified as "five o'clock"; and a beautiful woman with a Slavic appearance. A person identified as "three o'clock," who possesses a well-bred British accent, also attends. The group discusses the absence of "two o'clock" and the mysterious "seven o'clock," the leader who called the meeting but has never been seen.

The members express concern regarding "danger" and the possibility of people discovering their location and the "chimney house." Mosgolovsky emphasizes the need to silence suspicious individuals. The meeting shifts to business regarding a "two-footed dragon church" and a guest list that includes figures such as Sir Stanley Digby and Countess Anna Razky. They discuss the immense commercial and international value of an invention by Herr Eberhard.

The conversation reveals that a letter from Gerry Wade mentioning their organization was discovered by Miss Irene Brent, a failure attributed to someone named Bourne. Additionally, they confirm that the death of Ronnie De Vere has been successfully covered up as an accident involving a local boy. After the meeting concludes with a salute to "Anna," the members depart through a hidden door. Two hours later, Alfred arrives to release a pale and anxious Bondell.
